data.content_ref: inline:The forbidden case is too narrow to genuinely test the claim. The claim asserts a 'rapid, region-wide hyperarid climate shift' causing agricultural failures across the Eastern Mediterranean, yet the only forbidden case offered is a single speleothem record from Anatolia showing no drought. A single negative proxy from one region would not refute a region-wide hypothesis if other proxies across the covered regions showed drought—the hypothesis could survive by arguing Anatolia was a local exception. To genuinely risk refutation, the forbidden case should specify that multiple high-resolution proxies across the covered regions (Greece, Anatolia, Levant) simultaneously show no significant drought, or that the preponderance of paleoclimatic evidence contradicts a synchronous event.
